name: GLM-4.6
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 357B total / 32B active, 200K context (up from 128K in GLM-4.5), hybrid thinking/non-thinking response modes
note: Flagship coding/agentic update to GLM-4.5; expanded context to 200K, ~15% more token-efficient, near-parity with Claude Sonnet 4 on real-world coding (CC-Bench).

results.notes: AIME 2025, standard (non-tool) mode; Z.ai's own launch figure, relayed via Communeify/cirra.ai
LiveCodeBench v6; corroborated separately by CometAPI's write-up of the same launch figure
Terminal-Bench; exact harness version (2.0 vs original) not specified in source
tau2-Bench agentic tool-use benchmark, labeled 'T2-Bench' in source

verificationNotes: Confirmed real, released model (not a rumor) via Z.ai's own blog (z.ai/blog/glm-4.6, referenced but JS-rendered so not directly fetchable), Hugging Face model card (zai-org/GLM-4.6, MIT license, 357B/32B active, BF16/F32), and official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-4.6 (200K context, 128K max output). Release date 2025-09-30 corroborated by CometAPI, docs.z.ai release-notes page, and multiple independent outlets (IntuitionLabs, CodeGPT). Params: HF card and llm-stats.com both say 357B total/32B active; some secondary blogs round to '355B' -- treated as the same figure, minor rounding noise. License: HF card and llm-stats.com say MIT; two lower-quality secondary blogs (codegpt.co, intuitionlabs.ai) incorrectly say Apache 2.0 -- MIT from the primary HF card is authoritative. Benchmark numbers (AIME-25 93.9, GPQA 81.0, LiveCodeBench v6 82.8, BrowseComp 45.1, SWE-bench Verified 68.0, Terminal-Bench 40.5, tau2-Bench 75.9) come from a table on cirra.ai attributed to a Communeify.com relay of Z.ai's own launch announcement; the AIME-25 93.9 and LiveCodeBench-v6 82.8 figures were independently corroborated by a separate WebSearch synthesis and by CometAPI's 82.8 LiveCodeBench figure, giving reasonable confidence these are the vendor's real self-reported numbers even though I could not load the z.ai blog page directly (client-rendered SPA returned empty content to WebFetch). Treated as self-reported since ultimately vendor-originated. Did not include CC-Bench win-rate (48.6% vs Claude Sonnet 4), HumanEval 84.2, or MBPP 80.7 -- CC-Bench is a proprietary Zhipu win-rate metric with no standard slug, and the source article itself flags HumanEval/MBPP as 'from a Chinese technical blog without independent validation.' Terminal-Bench version (2.0 vs unversioned) is ambiguous in the source; mapped to base 'terminal-bench' slug rather than 'terminal-bench-2' to avoid over-claiming the harness version.
